Looking at the current state, it seems the board has returned to nearly the original state! Let me compare with the original configuration! The left green bar (ID 10) is now N (light green) and the right green bar (ID 28) is now N (light green). The red shape (ID 27) is back to original. Wait, let me look more carefully. The initial state had:
- ID 0: N, ID 27: R (red shape on left)
- ID 27: N (green shape on right)
Current state has:
- ID 27: R (red shape on left) 
- ID 28: N (green shape on right)
So clicking on the red shape permuted red shape (which was originally green) reversed the permutation back to original. So the mechanic seems to be:
1. Clicking on red shape -> permutes all colors
2. Clicking on red again -> reverses back
But wait, the second click was on what was the green shape (which became red after the first click). So it seems like clicking on the red shape always-red shape toggles the permutation.
Actually, let me reconsidering: the first click was on the red shape (left side). After that, the red shape became blue, and the green shape became red. Then I clicked on the red shape (which was originally the green shape became red). After that, everything went back to original.
So the mechanic is: clicking on the red shape toggles a color permutation. Clicking on red again reverses it.
But what is the goal? Maybe the goal is to get all the colored blocks to match some target configuration. Let me think about this is a puzzle where we need to match a target pattern.
Let me look at the blocks and the frame pattern. The blocks form a rectangular frame with colored blocks around the perimeter. The red and green shapes are the "player" or "cursor" that can move between positions.

Actually, looking at the image again, the red shape is still on the left and green shape is still on the right. So the positions haven't changed.
Let me think about what the goal might be. The blocks form a rectangular frame. Maybe the goal is to make the colors match some pattern.
Let me look at the top row of blocks:
Initial: g, S, b, p, Y, g
Left column: S, p, g, w
Bottom row: w, w, b, b, S, p, g
Right column: g, p, b, S
Wait, let me look at the frame more carefully. The blocks form a rectangular loop around in a specific order. Let me trace the frame:
Top row (left to right): [g, S, b, p, Y, g]
Right side (top to bottom): g, p, b, S
Bottom row (right to left): g, p, S, b, b, w, w
Left side (bottom to top): w, g, p, S
So the frame has 17 blocks total. Let me look at the colors in order:
Starting from top-left going clockwise: g, S, b, p, Y, g, p, b, S, g, p, b, S, g, w, w, b, b, S, p, g, w
Hmm, this is getting complex. Let me think about it differently.
Maybe the goal is to make all blocks the same color, or to match the colors in some symmetric pattern.
Let me try clicking on the red shape again to trigger the permutation, then look at what the permuted state to see if it creates a more uniform or patterned state.
Actually, let me look at the small yellow markers in the upper-left. These might be the target configuration! The small yellow blocks (IDs 12, 13, 22, 24) might indicate what the blocks should be.
Let me look at their positions:
- ID 12: Y, boundary [[17, 10], [17, 11], [18, 11], [18, 10]] - small 2x2 at top-left area
- ID 13: Y, boundary [[17, 16], [17, 17], [18, 17], [18, 16]] - small 2x2
- ID 22: Y, boundary [[23, 10], [23, 11], [24, 11], [24, 10]] - small 2x2
- ID 24: Y, boundary [[23, 16], [23, 17], [24, 17], [24, 16]] - small 2x2
These four small yellow blocks form a 2x2 at positions. They might be "markers" or "targets.
Let me also look at the gray blocks:
- ID 14: w, 16 pixels, [[19, 12], [19, 15], [22, 15], [22, 12]] - 4x4 block
- ID 15: c, 8 pixels, [[19, 16], [19, 17], [22, 17], [22, 16]] - 2x4 block
- ID 23: c, 8 pixels, [[23, 12], [23, 15], [24, 15], [24, 12]] - 2x4 block
These form a small mechanism in the upper-left area.
I think the small yellow markers and gray blocks form some kind of control panel. The four yellow blocks might be "buttons" that, when clicked, do something specific.
Let me try clicking on one of the yellow blocks to see what happens.
